coachNote: Yesterday told me a lot. Your scales are landing — but the black keys (B♭/E♭/G♭) sat right at the working edge, so today they move hands-together, where the real coordination lives, and the left hand gets its own slow reps to earn it. You found the chords and progressions too easy, so we step up for real: seventh chords, the ii–V–I as proper voicings, an arpeggio, and the pop loop both in a new key and hands-together. Minor gets a new colour too — A harmonic minor's raised 7th. Rate honestly and tell me what bites.

exercises.label: C major — hands together
exercises.cue: Quick wake-up. Even and loose; you own this one.

exercises.label: G major — hands together
exercises.cue: Same home shape. One pass each way, then move on.

exercises.label: B♭ major — hands together
exercises.block: Black-key scales · today they go together
exercises.cue: A touch faster than yesterday. Slip? Stop, fix the one crossing, carry on.

exercises.label: E♭ major — hands together
exercises.block: Black-key scales · today they go together
exercises.cue: Thumbs only on white keys. Keep the long fingers up on the black ones.

exercises.label: G♭ major — hands together
exercises.block: Black-key scales · today they go together
exercises.cue: The trickiest hand shape — almost all black keys. Slow is fine; clean is the goal.

exercises.label: B♭ major — left hand alone
exercises.block: Black-key scales · today they go together
exercises.cue: Left hand is the weak link in HT — earn it alone first. Lead the 1→4 pivot.

exercises.label: E♭ major — left hand alone
exercises.block: Black-key scales · today they go together
exercises.cue: Same idea in E♭. Thumb passes under to a white key every time.

exercises.label: A♭ major — right hand (new)
exercises.block: Black-key scales · today they go together
exercises.cue: New scale. Four flats, but the hand shape is friendly — start on finger 3, thumb on C and F.

exercises.label: A natural minor — hands together
exercises.cue: You found this easy hands-separate — now together. Same white keys, darker mood.

exercises.label: A harmonic minor — right hand
exercises.cue: Raise the 7th to G♯ — that big step from F to G♯ is the sound of harmonic minor. Listen for it.

exercises.label: ii–V–I sevenths — Dm7 · G7 · Cmaj7
exercises.cue: Four notes now, not three. Let each seventh ring — hear the colour the extra note adds.
exercises.fingering: RH 1 2 3 5 on each — the top note is the 7th

exercises.label: C inversions — hands together
exercises.abc:
X:1
M:4/4
L:1/1
K:C
V:1 clef=treble
V:2 clef=bass
[V:1] "C"[CEG] | "C/E"[EGc] | "C/G"[Gce] |]
[V:2] C, | E, | G, |]
exercises.cue: Right hand walks the inversions up; left hand names the bass. This is how slash chords work.
exercises.fingering: RH 1 3 5 each shape · LH plays the single bass note

exercises.label: C major arpeggio — right hand
exercises.cue: Break the chord into a line. Smooth, even; let the thumb pass under cleanly going up.

exercises.label: I–V–vi–IV in G — new key
exercises.cue: Same four-chord loop you owned in C — now in G (one sharp). Find the shapes by feel.

exercises.label: I–V–vi–IV in C — hands together
exercises.abc:
X:1
M:4/4
L:1/1
K:C
V:1 clef=treble
V:2 clef=bass
[V:1] "C"[CEG] | "G"[GBd] | "Am"[Ace] | "F"[FAc] |]
[V:2] C,, | G,, | A,, | F,, |]
exercises.cue: Right-hand triads over a left-hand root. This is an actual accompaniment — play it like a song.
exercises.fingering: RH triads 1 3 5 · LH single root under each
